Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class SystemStatusProvider

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Provides observable system status tracking with soft switch response integration.

public sealed class SystemStatusProvider : ISystemStatusProvider, ISoftSwitchResponder
Inheritance
object
SystemStatusProvider
Implements
ISystemStatusProvider
ISoftSwitchResponder
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Dual Role: This class serves two roles in the emulator architecture:

  1. ISystemStatusProvider - Read-only observable access for consumers (renderers, debuggers, UI) to query and subscribe to system state changes.
  2. ISoftSwitchResponder - Write interface for the bus/soft switch manager to update state when soft switches are toggled by programs.

Observable Pattern: Uses System.Reactive.Subjects.BehaviorSubject<T> to provide reactive updates via Stream. The BehaviorSubject replays the current state to new subscribers, ensuring they always have the latest snapshot. Traditional .NET events are also supported via Changed.

Default State: Initializes to Apple IIe power-on defaults:

  • Text mode enabled
  • Internal ROM enabled (INTCXROM = true)
  • All other switches off
This matches the behavior of a freshly powered-on Apple IIe before the monitor runs.

Thread Safety: Updates via Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>) are not inherently thread-safe. The caller (typically VA2MBus) must ensure serialized access from a single thread. Reads are safe from any thread due to immutable snapshots.

Change Notification Order: When state changes, the observable stream (Stream) is notified first, followed by the Changed event. This ensures reactive subscribers see updates before event handlers.

Constructors

SystemStatusProvider()

Initializes a new instance of the SystemStatusProvider class.

public SystemStatusProvider()

Remarks

Creates the reactive subject with the default power-on state, ensuring new subscribers immediately receive the current system status.

Properties

Current

Gets the current system status as an immutable snapshot.

public SystemStatusSnapshot Current { get; }

Property Value

SystemStatusSnapshot

A SystemStatusSnapshot containing all current soft switch states and system flags.

Remarks

This property provides a point-in-time snapshot of the entire system state, useful for comparing states, logging, or passing to components that need multiple status values atomically.

State80Store

Gets the state of the 80STORE soft switch ($C000/$C001).

public bool State80Store { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if 80STORE is enabled (page 2 redirected to auxiliary memory); false otherwise.

Remarks

When enabled, page 2 video memory is redirected to auxiliary RAM, enabling 80-column text mode and double hi-res graphics with independent video pages.

StateAltCharSet

Gets the state of the ALTCHAR soft switch ($C00E/$C00F).

public bool StateAltCharSet { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if using MouseText character set; false if using standard.

Remarks

Selects between the standard Apple II character set and the alternate MouseText character set. MouseText provides graphical symbols for building text-based UIs.

StateAltZp

Gets the state of the ALTZP soft switch ($C008/$C009).

public bool StateAltZp { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if using auxiliary zero page/stack; false if using main.

Remarks

Controls whether zero page ($0000-$00FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) access auxiliary memory. Allows programs to maintain separate CPU contexts.

StateAnn0

Gets the state of annunciator 0 ($C058/$C059).

public bool StateAnn0 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if annunciator 0 is on; false if off.

Remarks

General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software. Some games use annunciators for audio effects or peripheral control.

StateAnn1

Gets the state of annunciator 1 ($C05A/$C05B).

public bool StateAnn1 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if annunciator 1 is on; false if off.

Remarks

General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software.

StateAnn2

Gets the state of annunciator 2 ($C05C/$C05D).

public bool StateAnn2 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if annunciator 2 is on; false if off.

Remarks

General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software.

StateAnn3_DGR

Gets the state of annunciator 3 / double hi-res enable ($C05E/$C05F).

public bool StateAnn3_DGR { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if annunciator 3 is on; false if off.

Remarks

Annunciator 3 doubles as the double hi-res (DHR) enable switch.

Important: The logic is inverted for DHGR - when this value is false (annunciator off), double hi-res mode is enabled. When this value is true (annunciator on), double hi-res is disabled.

StateFlashOn

Gets the current flash state for flashing characters.

public bool StateFlashOn { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if flash characters should be displayed in inverse; false for normal.

Remarks

The Apple IIe automatically alternates the display of flashing characters (codes $40-$7F) between normal and inverse video at approximately 2 Hz. This property reflects the current phase of that cycle.

StateHiRes

Gets the state of the HIRES soft switch ($C056/$C057).

public bool StateHiRes { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if hi-res graphics mode; false if lo-res graphics mode.

Remarks

In graphics mode (TEXT off), controls whether to display hi-res graphics (280×192 pixels, 6 colors) or lo-res graphics (40×48 color blocks, 16 colors). Has no effect when TEXT mode is enabled.

StateHighRead

Gets the language card read enable state.

public bool StateHighRead { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if reading from language card RAM; false if reading from ROM.

Remarks

Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF access the language card RAM or the built-in ROM. Independent of write enable, allowing read-from-ROM, write-to-RAM configurations.

StateHighWrite

Gets the language card write enable state.

public bool StateHighWrite { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if writing to language card RAM is enabled; false if write-protected.

Remarks

Controls whether writes to the $D000-$FFFF range modify the language card RAM. Write protection is engaged through a two-access sequence to prevent accidental modification of critical code.

StateIntCxRom

Gets the state of the INTCXROM soft switch ($C006/$C007).

public bool StateIntCxRom { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if using internal ROM; false if using slot ROMs.

Remarks

Controls whether the $C100-$CFFF range accesses internal ROM or peripheral card slot ROMs. Typically set by the monitor during initialization.

StateMixed

Gets the state of the MIXED soft switch ($C052/$C053).

public bool StateMixed { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if mixed mode enabled; false if full-screen mode.

Remarks

When true and in graphics mode, displays graphics on the top 20 rows (160 scanlines) and text on the bottom 4 rows (32 scanlines). Commonly used in games to show graphics with a text status line. Has no effect in text mode.

StatePage2

Gets the state of the PAGE2 soft switch ($C054/$C055).

public bool StatePage2 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if displaying page 2; false if displaying page 1.

Remarks

Selects which video page is displayed. Page 1 uses $0400-$07FF (text) or $2000-$3FFF (hi-res). Page 2 uses $0800-$0BFF (text) or $4000-$5FFF (hi-res). Can be used for page flipping animation.

StatePb0

Gets the state of pushbutton 0 ($C061).

public bool StatePb0 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if button 0 is pressed; false if released.

Remarks

Represents the state of game controller button 0. The high bit of the byte read from $C061 indicates the button state.

StatePb1

Gets the state of pushbutton 1 ($C062).

public bool StatePb1 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if button 1 is pressed; false if released.

Remarks

Represents the state of game controller button 1. The high bit of the byte read from $C062 indicates the button state.

StatePb2

Gets the state of pushbutton 2 ($C063).

public bool StatePb2 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if button 2 is pressed; false if released.

Remarks

Represents the state of game controller button 2. The high bit of the byte read from $C063 indicates the button state.

StatePreWrite

Gets the language card pre-write state.

public bool StatePreWrite { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if the pre-write sequence is active; false otherwise.

Remarks

The language card uses a two-access sequence to enable writing. The first access sets pre-write mode. A second consecutive access to the same address enables write mode. This is an internal state for the write protection mechanism.

StateRamRd

Gets the state of the RAMRD soft switch ($C002/$C003).

public bool StateRamRd { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if reading from auxiliary memory; false if reading from main memory.

Remarks

Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is used for memory reads. Allows programs to access the Apple IIe's extended 128KB memory space.

StateRamWrt

Gets the state of the RAMWRT soft switch ($C004/$C005).

public bool StateRamWrt { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if writing to auxiliary memory; false if writing to main memory.

Remarks

Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is used for memory writes. Independent from RAMRD, allowing asymmetric read/write configurations.

StateShow80Col

Gets the state of the 80VID soft switch ($C00C/$C00D).

public bool StateShow80Col { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if 80-column mode enabled; false if 40-column mode.

Remarks

Enables 80-column text mode, which uses both main and auxiliary memory to display 80 characters per line (alternating bytes from main and aux). When false, standard 40-column text mode is used.

StateSlotC3Rom

Gets the state of the SLOTC3ROM soft switch ($C00A/$C00B).

public bool StateSlotC3Rom { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if using slot C3 ROM; false if using internal ROM.

Remarks

Controls whether the $C300-$C3FF range (slot 3) uses peripheral card ROM or internal ROM. Independent of INTCXROM. Commonly used for 80-column card firmware.

StateTextMode

Gets the state of the TEXT soft switch ($C050/$C051).

public bool StateTextMode { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if text mode; false if graphics mode.

Remarks

Controls the primary video mode. When true, displays 40-column or 80-column text. When false, displays lo-res or hi-res graphics, depending on the HIRES switch.

StateUseBank1

Gets the language card bank selection state.

public bool StateUseBank1 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True if bank 1 is selected; false if bank 2 is selected.

Remarks

The language card provides two 4KB RAM banks for the $D000-$DFFF address space. Bank 1 is physically located at $C000-$CFFF, while bank 2 is at $D000-$DFFF.

Stream

Gets an observable stream of system status snapshots.

public IObservable<SystemStatusSnapshot> Stream { get; }

Property Value

IObservable<SystemStatusSnapshot>

An IObservable<T> that emits snapshots whenever system status changes.

Remarks

This observable stream provides the same notifications as the Changed event, but in a reactive (Rx.NET) format. Useful for composing reactive pipelines, throttling updates, or combining with other observables.

Example usage: 

statusProvider.Stream
    .Throttle(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(16))
    .ObserveOn(RxApp.MainThreadScheduler)
    .Subscribe(snapshot => UpdateUI(snapshot));

Methods

Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>)

Internal mutation method for updating the status snapshot.

public void Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder> mutator)

Parameters

mutator Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>

A builder action that modifies the snapshot state

Remarks

For internal use by emulator core only. This method is called by the bus or soft switch manager to update the system status when switches are toggled. It uses a builder pattern to efficiently update the immutable snapshot and notify subscribers.

Consumers should not call this method - use the read-only properties and subscribe to Changed or Stream instead.

Set80Store(bool)

Called when the 80STORE soft switch is changed ($C000/$C001).

public void Set80Store(bool store80)

Parameters

store80 bool

True if 80STORE is enabled; false if disabled

Remarks

When enabled, page 2 memory ($0800-$0BFF for text, $2000-$3FFF for hi-res) is redirected to auxiliary memory when PAGE2 is set. This is used for 80-column text mode and double hi-res graphics to provide independent main and aux video pages.

Set80Vid(bool)

Called when the 80VID soft switch is changed ($C00C/$C00D).

public void Set80Vid(bool vid)

Parameters

vid bool

True for 80-column mode; false for 40-column mode

Remarks

Enables 80-column text mode, which uses both main and auxiliary memory to display 80 characters per line (alternating bytes from main and aux memory). When false, standard 40-column text mode is used.

SetAltChar(bool)

Called when the ALTCHAR soft switch is changed ($C00E/$C00F).

public void SetAltChar(bool altChar)

Parameters

altChar bool

True for MouseText character set; false for standard

Remarks

Selects between the standard Apple II character set and the alternate MouseText character set. MouseText provides graphical symbols for building text-based UIs (boxes, arrows, icons) in the $40-$5F character range.

SetAltZp(bool)

Called when the ALTZP soft switch is changed ($C008/$C009).

public void SetAltZp(bool altZp)

Parameters

altZp bool

True to use auxiliary zero page/stack; false for main

Remarks

Controls whether zero page ($0000-$00FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) access main or auxiliary memory. When true, these critical CPU regions use auxiliary memory, allowing programs to maintain separate zero page and stack contexts.

SetAn0(bool)

Called when annunciator 0 is changed ($C058/$C059).

public void SetAn0(bool an0)

Parameters

an0 bool

True if annunciator 0 is on; false if off

Remarks

Annunciator 0 is a general-purpose output signal. Originally intended for controlling external devices, rarely used in standard software. Some games use annunciators for audio effects or peripheral control.

SetAn1(bool)

Called when annunciator 1 is changed ($C05A/$C05B).

public void SetAn1(bool an1)

Parameters

an1 bool

True if annunciator 1 is on; false if off

Remarks

Annunciator 1 is a general-purpose output signal. Originally intended for controlling external devices, rarely used in standard software.

SetAn2(bool)

Called when annunciator 2 is changed ($C05C/$C05D).

public void SetAn2(bool an2)

Parameters

an2 bool

True if annunciator 2 is on; false if off

Remarks

Annunciator 2 is a general-purpose output signal. Originally intended for controlling external devices, rarely used in standard software.

SetAn3(bool)

Called when annunciator 3 is changed ($C05E/$C05F).

public void SetAn3(bool an3)

Parameters

an3 bool

True if annunciator 3 is on; false if off

Remarks

Annunciator 3 doubles as the double hi-res (DHR) enable switch.

Important: The logic is inverted - when this annunciator is off (false), double hi-res mode is enabled. When the annunciator is on (true), double hi-res is disabled. This backwards behavior is a quirk of the Apple IIe hardware design.

Double hi-res mode (560×192 pixels, 16 colors) uses both main and auxiliary video pages. When not used for DHGR control, this functions as a general-purpose output signal like the other annunciators.

SetBank1(bool)

Called when the language card bank selection changes ($C080-$C08F).

public void SetBank1(bool enabled)

Parameters

enabled bool

True for bank 1 (D000-DFFF physical at C000); false for bank 2 (at D000)

Remarks

The language card provides two 4KB RAM banks that can be mapped into the $D000-$DFFF address space. Bank 1 is physically located at $C000-$CFFF, while bank 2 is at $D000-$DFFF. This is often confusing because bank 1's physical location overlaps with I/O space in the address map.

SetHiRes(bool)

Called when the HIRES soft switch is changed ($C056/$C057).

public void SetHiRes(bool hires)

Parameters

hires bool

True for hi-res graphics; false for lo-res graphics

Remarks

In graphics mode (TEXT off), controls whether to display hi-res graphics (280×192 pixels, 6 colors) or lo-res graphics (40×48 color blocks, 16 colors). Has no effect when TEXT mode is enabled.

SetHighRead(bool)

Called when the language card read enable state changes ($C080-$C08F).

public void SetHighRead(bool enabled)

Parameters

enabled bool

True to read from language card RAM; false to read from ROM

Remarks

Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF access the language card RAM or the built-in ROM. When true, the selected bank ($D000-$DFFF) and upper 8KB ($E000-$FFFF) are read from RAM. When false, ROM is visible instead. Independent of write enable, allowing read-from-ROM, write-to-RAM configurations.

SetHighWrite(bool)

Called when the language card write enable state changes ($C080-$C08F).

public void SetHighWrite(bool enabled)

Parameters

enabled bool

True if writing to language card RAM is enabled; false if write-protected

Remarks

Controls whether writes to the $D000-$FFFF range modify the language card RAM or are ignored. Write protection is engaged through a two-access sequence to prevent accidental modification of critical code. When false, writes to this region have no effect (ROM remains visible during reads).

SetIntCxRom(bool)

Called when the INTCXROM soft switch is changed ($C006/$C007).

public void SetIntCxRom(bool intCxRom)

Parameters

intCxRom bool

True to use internal ROM; false to use slot ROMs

Remarks

Controls whether the $C100-$CFFF range accesses internal ROM or peripheral card slot ROMs. When true (internal), the built-in ROM is used. When false, individual slot ROMs can be accessed. Typically set by the monitor during initialization.

SetMixed(bool)

Called when the MIXED soft switch is changed ($C052/$C053).

public void SetMixed(bool mixed)

Parameters

mixed bool

True for mixed mode; false for full-screen mode

Remarks

When true and in graphics mode, displays graphics on the top 20 rows (160 scanlines) and text on the bottom 4 rows (32 scanlines). Commonly used in games to show graphics with a text status line. Has no effect in text mode.

SetPage2(bool)

Called when the PAGE2 soft switch is changed ($C054/$C055).

public void SetPage2(bool page2)

Parameters

page2 bool

True for page 2; false for page 1

Remarks

Selects which video page is displayed. Page 1 uses $0400-$07FF (text) or $2000-$3FFF (hi-res). Page 2 uses $0800-$0BFF (text) or $4000-$5FFF (hi-res). Can be used for page flipping animation. Interaction with 80STORE affects whether page 2 uses main or auxiliary memory.

SetPreWrite(bool)

Called when the language card pre-write state changes ($C080-$C08F).

public void SetPreWrite(bool enabled)

Parameters

enabled bool

True if pre-write sequence is active; false otherwise

Remarks

The language card uses a two-access sequence to enable writing, preventing accidental modification. The first access to certain addresses sets pre-write mode. A second consecutive access to the same address enables write mode. Any other memory access clears pre-write. This is an internal state used to implement the write protection mechanism.

SetRamRd(bool)

Called when the RAMRD soft switch is changed ($C002/$C003).

public void SetRamRd(bool ramRd)

Parameters

ramRd bool

True to read from auxiliary memory; false to read from main memory

Remarks

Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is read from for memory accesses. This affects most memory regions except those controlled by other soft switches (like 80STORE for video pages). Used for accessing the extended 128KB memory space.

SetRamWrt(bool)

Called when the RAMWRT soft switch is changed ($C004/$C005).

public void SetRamWrt(bool ramWrt)

Parameters

ramWrt bool

True to write to auxiliary memory; false to write to main memory

Remarks

Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is written to for memory accesses. Independent from RAMRD, allowing asymmetric configurations (e.g., read from main, write to aux). Used for accessing the extended 128KB memory space.

SetSlotC3Rom(bool)

Called when the SLOTC3ROM soft switch is changed ($C00A/$C00B).

public void SetSlotC3Rom(bool slotC3Rom)

Parameters

slotC3Rom bool

True to use slot C3 ROM; false to use internal ROM

Remarks

Controls whether the $C300-$C3FF range (slot 3) uses the peripheral card ROM or internal ROM. Independent of INTCXROM, allowing slot 3 to be controlled separately from other slots. Commonly used for 80-column card firmware.

SetText(bool)

Called when the TEXT soft switch is changed ($C050/$C051).

public void SetText(bool text)

Parameters

text bool

True for text mode; false for graphics mode

Remarks

Controls the primary video mode. When true, displays 40-column or 80-column text. When false, displays lo-res (40×48 color blocks) or hi-res (280×192 pixels) graphics, depending on the HIRES switch.

Events

Changed

Raised when any system status changes.

public event EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>? Changed

Event Type

EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>

Remarks

This event fires whenever any soft switch or system flag changes state. The event args contain a SystemStatusSnapshot with the new state. Subscribers can use this for event-driven updates to UI or other components.

For reactive programming patterns, consider using Stream instead.

MemoryMappingChanged

Event raised when any soft switch affecting memory mapping changes.

public event EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>? MemoryMappingChanged

Event Type

EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>

Remarks

Fires only when RAMRD, RAMWRT, ALTZP, 80STORE, HIRES, PAGE2, INTCXROM, SLOTC3ROM, HIGHWRITE, BANK1, or HIGHREAD change. Provides the full SystemStatusSnapshot for convenience.
